title: lithocarpus havilandii
intro: Lithocarpus havilandii, also known as the Haviland's tanbark oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to Borneo. The bark of this plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, containing tannins and other compounds that have been found to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The medicinal properties of Lithocarpus havilandii have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems, as well as to alleviate symptoms of diarrhea and sore throats. In traditional herbal preparations, the bark is often used to make decoctions, teas, and infusions, which are used to harness the plant's medicinal properties for various health benefits.
